name: Router
comment:
A simple nested router for single page applications.

See [Example](https://github.com/apuchenkin/elm-nested-router/tree/master/example) ([Live demo](http://apuchenkin.github.io/elm-nested-router/example))
and [Tests](https://github.com/apuchenkin/elm-nested-router/tree/master/test/Test) for more details

@docs runRouter, initialState

values.name: initialState
values.comment: Initial state for router. Fed this into your application state

values.name: runRouter
values.type: RouterConfig route (WithRouter route state) -> RouterResult (WithRouter route state)

name: Router.Helpers
comment:
A set of utility functions
@docs singleton, noFx, combineParams, chainAction, doNothing, memoFallback

values.name: chainAction
values.type: Action state -> Action state -> Action state

values.name: combineParams
values.comment: Combine route wit a provided params

values.name: memoFallback
values.comment: Performs function memoization with a fallback

name: Router.Types

unions.name: Constraint
unions.comment: A constraint of route parameter type

unions.name: Response
unions.comment: An action result - a modified state combined with side effects

unions.name: RouterConfig
unions.comment:
`RouterConfig` is configuration for the router:

  * `init` — Initial application state
  * `html5` — Use html5 pushState.
  * `removeTrailingSlash` — Trailing slashes will be removed from matched and builded urls
  * `fallback` — A fallback route is used when url matching fails
  * `layout` — Main rendered function that combines named views gathered from Handlers in a single HTML
  * `onTransition` — An action that should be executed on every router transition
  * `routeConfig` — A mapping between route and route configuration
  * `routes` — A list of routes available for routing
  * `inits` — A list of signals that should run for inititialisation of state
  * `inputs` — A list of signals utilized in application in runtime
cases: { init : state, html5 : Bool, removeTrailingSlash : Bool, fallback : Route route, layout : Router route state -> state -> Dict String Html -> Html, onTransition : Router route state -> Transition route state, routeConfig : route -> RouteConfig route state, routes : List route, inits : List (Signal (Action state)), inputs : List (Signal (Action state)) }

aliases.name: Action
aliases.comment: `Action` represents function that prforms something with application state, and might contain side efects

aliases.name: ActionEffects
aliases.comment: Helper to get rid of brackets

aliases.name: Handler
aliases.comment:
A `Handler` is a piece of functionality binded to specific route
  * `view` — Function that describes how to render application state to map of named views
  * `actions` — A set of necessary to perform actions
aliases.type: { view : state -> Dict String Html -> Dict String Html, actions : List (Action state) }

aliases.name: Route
aliases.comment: combined abstract route type with params

aliases.name: RouteConfig
aliases.comment:
`RouteConfig` is a route configuration

  * `segment` — URL segment

  Expample:
```
"/home",
"/post/:postId",
"/author[/:authorId]"
```
  * `parent` — A parent route
  * `bypass` — When setted to True - route will not be matched directly, but still can provide actions and views
  * `constraints` — A set of constraints applied to route params. (`String`, `Int`, `Enum`, `Regexp`) constraints are supported
  * `handler` — A binding to handler.

  **Exapmle of route configuration**:
```
  config = {
    -- "author" and "postId" is dynamic url parts
    -- "postId" is marked as optional and might me ommited in URL
    segment = "/page/:author[/:postId]"
  , parent = Nothing
    -- setting a parent for route means that full route URL will be combined with it's parent, and actions for route and it's parent will be fired on match
  , bypass = False
  , constraints = Dict.fromList [("author", String),("postId", Int)]
    -- constraints specify that `author` param must be a string,
    -- and postId an integer
  , handler = always PostHandler
  }
```

  A `config` above will match following URLs:
```
"/page/mark/1", "/page/mark", "/page/joe"
```
  "mark" and "joe" will be stored as `author` param, and "1" as `postId`
  Everything enclosed by brackets considered as optional.
aliases.type: { segment : RawSegment, parent : Maybe route, bypass : Bool, constraints : Dict Param Constraint, handler : Router route state -> Handler state }

aliases.name: RouteParams
aliases.comment: A map of route param names and values

aliases.name: Router
aliases.comment:
A `Router` is a provider of following functions:

  * `bindForward` — Binds a `forward` action to a provided `Route` with a list of html attributes.
    This is useful to create links in application
  * `buildUrl` — Builds an URL for provided `Route`
  * `forward` — Preforms a transition to provided `Route`
  * `redirect` — Redirects to provided `Route`
  * `match` — Performs attempt to match provided URL.

  Router also provide it's `config` and `address`
aliases.type: { config : RouterConfig route state, address : Address (Action state), bindForward : Route route -> List Attribute -> List Attribute, buildUrl : Route route -> URL, forward : Route route -> Action state, redirect : Route route -> Action state, match : String -> Maybe (Route route) }

aliases.name: RouterResult
aliases.comment:
A `RouterResult` is a combination of resulting signals:

  * `html` — a signal of `Html` representing the current visual
    representation of your app. This should be fed into `main`.
  * `state` — a signal representing the central state of your application.
  * `tasks` — a signal of tasks that need to get run. Your app is going
    to be producing tasks in response to all sorts of events, so this needs to
    be hooked up to a `port` to ensure they get run.
aliases.type: { html : Signal Html, state : Signal state, tasks : Signal (Task Never ()) }

aliases.name: Transition
aliases.comment: A transition from route A to route B
aliases.type: Maybe (Route route) -> Route route -> Action state

aliases.name: WithRouter
aliases.comment: Type extension for the application state
